title: Stone Removal Game
content:
Alice and Bob are playing a game where they take turns removing stones from a pile, with Alice going first.

• Alice starts by removing exactly 10 stones on her first turn.

• For each subsequent turn, each player removes exactly 1 fewer stone than the previous opponent.

The player who cannot make a move loses the game.

Given a positive integer n, return true if Alice wins the game and false otherwise.

Example 1:

Input: n = 12

Output: true

Explanation:

• Alice removes 10 stones on her first turn, leaving 2 stones for Bob.

• Bob cannot remove 9 stones, so Alice wins.

Example 2:

Input: n = 1

Output: false

Explanation:

• Alice cannot remove 10 stones, so Alice loses.

Constraints:

• 1 <= n <= 50
hints: The constraints are small enough that a brute-force solution is feasible.

solutions.explanation: Simulate: Alice starts by removing 10, then each turn decreases by 1; the player who cannot move loses. O(sqrt(n)) time.
